title: Searching Across Folders
subtitle: Find text in any file with grep -r

project.readme.txt:
This is my project.
It runs on port 3000.
See config.txt for settings.

sections.instruction: What if you don't know which file contains the text? Search ALL files at once. The `-r` flag tells grep to search recursively through every file in a folder.
sections.prompt: Type `grep -r "port" .` to search recursively for 'port' starting from the current directory.

onSuccess.message: Found 'port' in three different files! `-r` means recursive — search this folder and all subfolders. The `.` means 'start here'. You didn't need to know which file had the text.

sections.instruction: Now search for 'localhost' across all files in the project.
sections.prompt: Type `grep -r "localhost" .` to find every mention of localhost.

onSuccess.message: Found in app.js and config.txt. In real projects with hundreds of files across dozens of folders, `grep -r` is a lifesaver. You'll use it every day.

completionMessage: `grep -r` is one of the most used commands in professional development. When you join a new codebase with thousands of files, it's how you find where things are defined, where functions are called, and where bugs are hiding.
